creator: tofu.dev@blue.gismo.ca
text:
gnome-power-manager 2.24.4_4 fails to install with an unknown build error using portinstall.

When doing a make from /usr/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager, it fails running docbook2man.  docbook2man can be found in /usr/local/bin/docbook2man (so the bin already exists).

After perusing the FreeBSD Ports on docbook ports, on a whim I installed docbook-to-man (since it was missing).

Installed docbook ports prior to installaing docbook-to-man:
---
ciel# portversion -Fv "*docbook*"
docbook-1.4                 =  up-to-date with port
docbook-3.1_4               =  up-to-date with port
docbook-4.1_3               =  up-to-date with port
docbook-4.2                 =  up-to-date with port
docbook-4.3                 =  up-to-date with port
docbook-4.4_2               =  up-to-date with port
docbook-4.5_2               =  up-to-date with port
docbook-5.0_1               =  up-to-date with port
docbook-sk-4.1.2_4          =  up-to-date with port
docbook-utils-0.6.14_7      =  up-to-date with port
docbook-xml-4.2_1           =  up-to-date with port
docbook-xml-4.3             =  up-to-date with port
docbook-xml-4.4_1           =  up-to-date with port
docbook-xml-4.5             =  up-to-date with port
docbook-xsl-1.74.0_1        =  up-to-date with port
dsssl-docbook-modular-1.79_1,1  =  up-to-date with port
sdocbook-xml-1.1,1          =  up-to-date with port
---

Snapshot of gnome-power-manager build error from make:
---
Making all in docs
gmake[2]: Entering directory `/disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/docs'
gmake[2]: Nothing to be done for `all'.
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/docs'
Making all in man
gmake[2]: Entering directory `/disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/man'
docbook2man gnome-power-preferences.sgml > gnome-power-preferences.1
gmake[2]: *** [gnome-power-preferences.1] Error 8
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/man'
gmake[1]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4'
gmake: *** [all] Error 2
*** Error code 1

Stop in /disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager.
---

Output of manual rerun of docbook2man command:
---
ciel# docbook2man work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/man/gnome-power-preferences.sgml
Using catalogs: /usr/local/share/sgml/catalog
Using stylesheet: /usr/local/share/sgml/docbook/utils-0.6.14/docbook-utils.dsl#print
Working on: /disk1/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager/work/gnome-power-manager-2.24.4/man/gnome-power-preferences.sgml
Can't locate SGMLS/Output.pm in @INC (@INC contains: /usr/local/lib/perl5/5.8.9/BSDPAN /usr/local/lib/perl5/site_perl/5.8.9/mach /usr/local/lib/perl5/site_perl/5.8.9 /usr/local/lib/perl5/5.8.9/mach /usr/local/lib/perl5/5.8.9 .) at /usr/local/bin/sgmlspl line 56.
BEGIN failed--compilation aborted at /usr/local/bin/sgmlspl line 56.
---

Fix: 

So it looks (to me) like there's a dependency on docbook-to-man (or something that docbook-to-man installs).

Running an install of docbook2man results in:
---
--->  Installation of textproc/docbook-to-man ended at: Sat, 09 May 2009 04:19:58 -0700 (consumed 00:00:14)
--->  Fresh installation of textproc/docbook-to-man ended at: Sat, 09 May 2009 04:19:58 -0700 (consumed 00:00:37)
[Updating the pkgdb <format:bdb_btree> in /var/db/pkg ... - 885 packages found (-0 +1) . done]
--->  ** Install tasks 4: 4 done, 0 ignored, 0 skipped and 0 failed
--->  Listing the results (+:done / -:ignored / *:skipped / !:failed)
        + x11/xorg-cf-files
        + devel/gccmakedep
        + devel/imake
        + textproc/docbook-to-man
--->  Packages processed: 4 done, 0 ignored, 0 skipped and 0 failed
--->  Session ended at: Sat, 09 May 2009 04:20:27 -0700 (consumed 00:05:34)
---
How-To-Repeat: 1) Ensure that gnome-power-manager is not installed.
2) Ensure that docbook-to-man is not installed.
3) in /usr/ports/sysutils/gnome-power-manager, do make.
4) You should see the problem outlined above.
